docs.title: KR2202
docs.text:
Question 01. Why can my remote control Relay 1 but not Relay 2?

Answer:

Your setup is wrong, so your remote can only control Relay 1. The receiver is not broken.

You need to pair it again with the steps below.

  1. Press the Learning button 8 times to reset.
  2. Press the Learning button 1 time for Momentary mode.
  3. Press the button for Relay 1. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  4. Press the button for Relay 2. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

This sets both relays.

Question 02. Why do both button A and button B control Relay 1 only after Momentary pairing?

Answer:

Your pairing steps are wrong, so button A and button B both control Relay 1 only. The receiver is not broken.

You need to pair it again with the steps below.

  1. Press the Learning button 8 times to reset.
  2. Press the Learning button 1 time for Momentary mode.
  3. Press button A. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  4. Press button B. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

This sets both relays.

Question 03. Momentary, Toggle, and Latching mode. What is the difference?

Answer:

KR2202 has three working modes.

  • Momentary mode: hold the button, the relay turns on. Release the button, the relay turns off.
  • Toggle mode: press the button once, the relay turns on. Press the same button again, the relay turns off.
  • Latching mode: press button A, Relay 1 turns on. Press button B, Relay 2 turns on and Relay 1 turns off. Press the STOP button, the active relay turns off.

Use Momentary mode for hold-to-run control. Use Toggle mode for on / off control. Use Latching mode when two directions must not turn on at the same time.

Question 04. Do the two relays trigger at the same time or one by one?

Answer:

No. KR2202 does not have an auto-sequence mode.

The two relays are controlled by different remote buttons. In Momentary mode or Toggle mode, button A controls Relay 1 and button B controls Relay 2.

Question 05. How do I set two remotes to control Relay 1 and Relay 2 on KR2202?

Answer:

Pair one button on the first remote to Relay 1. Pair one button on the second remote to Relay 2. Do both in the same setup.

Example for Toggle mode:

  1. Press the Learning button on the receiver 2 times. Wait until the light turns off.
  2. Press the button on the first remote for Relay 1. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  3. Press the button on the second remote for Relay 2. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

If the pairing is wrong, press the Learning button 8 times to reset. Reset will delete all paired remotes.

Question 06. Can I pair multiple remotes to channels A and B in Momentary mode?

Answer:

Yes. KR2202 can pair multiple remotes to channels A and B in Momentary mode.

For each new remote, pair both buttons in the same Momentary setup. Do not pair button A alone.

Press the Learning button 8 times only if you want to reset. Reset will delete all paired remotes.

Question 07. Why does my 3-button UP / STOP / DOWN remote not work for motor control in Toggle mode?

Answer:

Toggle mode is not the right mode for UP / STOP / DOWN motor control. Use Latching mode instead.

Pair UP and DOWN in the same Latching mode setup:

  1. Press the Learning button 8 times to reset.
  2. Press the Learning button 3 times to enter Latching mode.
  3. Press UP. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  4. Press DOWN. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

After this setup: - Press UP, the motor runs up. - Press DOWN, the motor runs down and UP turns off. - Press STOP, the active relay turns off.

For motor control, the up and down directions must not turn on at the same time.

Question 08. How do I use KR2202 Momentary mode to control a two-direction motor with button A and button B?

Answer:

Yes. You can use KR2202 Momentary mode for a two-direction motor.

  1. Press the Learning button 1 time. Wait until the light turns off.
  2. Press button A on the remote. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  3. Press button B on the remote. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

In Momentary mode, the relay turns on while you hold the button. For a two-direction motor, both directions must not turn on at the same time.

If the motor system has its own control input, connect KR2202 to that input. Do not connect KR2202 directly to the motor power wires.

Question 09. Does the KR2202 relay stay on after power loss?

Answer:

No. The relay cannot stay on when the power is off.

KR2202 can remember the paired remote and working mode after power loss. But the relay needs power to stay on. After power comes back, the next relay action depends on the mode and remote command.

Question 10. Is the KR2202 receiver powered by 220V, or are the relay contacts rated for 220V?

Answer:

Yes, the KR2202 receiver can be powered by 220V AC. But the relay contacts do not output 220V by themselves.

The relay output is a dry contact. This means it works like a switch. It does not give power by itself. The terminals are NO, COM, and NC.

Question 11. Is KR2202 compatible with 230V AC?

Answer:

Yes. KR2202 can work with 230V AC power.

Its input voltage range is AC 85V-250V, so 230V AC is within the supported range.

Question 12. How should I wire KR2202 for my load?

Answer:

It depends on what load you want to control.

Use the wiring diagram that matches your load, such as a motor, lamp, or other device. Turn off power before wiring. Do not reverse L and N. Keep the load within Max 1100W.

L and N power the KR2202 receiver itself. The relay terminals do not output power by themselves. They are dry contacts. They work like switches for the load circuit you connect.

Question 13. Can one KR2202 remote control two garage door motors?

Answer:

It depends on what you mean by garage door motor.

If you mean a garage door opener with a control input, yes. KR2202 can trigger the control input. It works like pressing a wall button.

Do not use KR2202 to drive bare garage door motors directly. KR2202 is a relay receiver, not a motor controller.

Question 14. Can KR2202 control a winch raise and lower motor?

Answer:

Yes, but only if the winch motor matches KR2202.

Check the motor voltage, running current, and starting current first. KR2202 input is AC 85V-250V. The rated load is Max 1100W. For raise and lower control, the up and down directions must not turn on at the same time.

Question 15. Is KR2202 suitable for a roller shutter or garage shutter motor?

Answer:

Yes. KR2202 can work for some roller shutter or garage shutter motors.

Key points:

  • The shutter motor or controller must match KR2202 power and load limits: AC 85V-250V input and Max 1100W load.
  • Momentary mode is a good choice. The motor runs while you hold the button.
  • If the shutter has a manual wall switch, connect KR2202 COM and NO across the switch terminals. This adds remote control.
Question 16. Can KR2202 use button A and button B for gate open and close in Momentary mode?

Answer:

Yes, if your gate controller has dry contact OPEN and CLOSE inputs.

Use Relay 1 for OPEN and Relay 2 for CLOSE. Set KR2202 to Momentary mode. The gate will respond while you hold the button. Connect KR2202 only to dry contact input terminals.

Question 17. How do I identify a 3-wire roller shutter motor? Does KR2202 stop at the end position?

Answer:

No. Do not assume the third wire is ground. KR2202 does not stop the shutter at the end position by itself.

A 3-wire shutter motor may be Live / Neutral / Ground, or it may be Common / Up / Down. Check the motor label or manual before wiring. KR2202 turns the relay on and off. The shutter motor or controller must have its own limit switch.

Question 18. Why does KR2202 work with no load but fail when I connect a pool cover motor?

Answer:

KR2202 is probably not the broken part. The problem is the motor load or the motor control method.

The two most likely reasons are:

  1. The motor starting current is too high. A motor needs more current when it starts than when it runs. Even if the running power is under Max 1100W, the starting current may be too high for KR2202. Do not use KR2202 to switch the motor power directly. Use a motor controller or contactor. Or check the starting current before wiring.

  2. The pool cover motor may need its own control input. KR2202 is a relay receiver, not a motor controller. It works like a switch through NO and COM. If the pool cover system has a wall switch or control input, connect KR2202 to that input. Do not switch the motor power wires directly.

Check the motor label or manual for starting current and control input type. If you are not sure, ask an electrician before wiring.

Question 19. Can one remote control two KR2202 receivers in Momentary mode and Toggle mode?

Answer:

Yes, it is possible, but we do not recommend it if the two receivers are close to each other.

One remote sends the same RF signal. If both receivers are in range, both will receive the signal and operate. Keep the two receivers far apart so they are not in the same range. Or use separate remotes.

Question 20. Can I add a signal amplifier to KR2202 to reach 100 meters?

Answer:

No. We do not recommend adding a signal amplifier to KR2202.

KR2202 uses 433.92MHz remote control. To improve distance, fully extend the receiver antenna. Keep it away from metal and motors. If you need about 100 meters, use a longer-range remote with an antenna. Do not change the receiver.

docs.text:

Your setup is wrong, so your remote can only control Relay 1. The receiver is not broken.

You need to pair it again with the steps below.

  1. Press the Learning button 8 times to reset.
  2. Press the Learning button 1 time for Momentary mode.
  3. Press the button for Relay 1. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.

Use Momentary mode for hold-to-run control. Use Toggle mode for on / off control. Use Latching mode when Relay 1 and Relay 2 must not stay on at the same time.

Press the Learning button 8 times only if you want to reset. Reset will delete all paired remotes.

Question 07. How do I use KR2402A Momentary mode to control a two-direction motor with button A and button B?

Answer:

Yes. You can use KR2402A Momentary mode for a two-direction motor.

  1. Press the Learning button 1 time. Wait until the light turns off.
  2. Press button A on the remote. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  3. Press button B on the remote. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

In Momentary mode, the relay turns on while you hold the button. For a two-direction motor, both directions must not turn on at the same time.

If the motor system has its own control input, connect KR2402A to that input. Do not connect KR2402A directly to the motor power wires.

Question 08. Does the KR2402A relay stay on after power loss?

Answer:

No. The relay cannot stay on when the power is off.

KR2402A can remember the paired remote and working mode after power loss. But the relay needs power to stay on. After power comes back, the next relay action depends on the mode and remote command.

Question 09. How should I wire KR2402A for my load?

Answer:

It depends on what load you want to control.

Use the wiring diagram that matches your load, such as a lamp, AC motor, or DC motor. Turn off power before wiring.

+V and -V power the KR2402A receiver itself. The receiver input is DC 5V-60V. For relay output wiring, use the correct terminals: NO1 / COM1 / NC1 for Relay 1, and NO2 / COM2 / NC2 for Relay 2.

The relay terminals do not output power by themselves. They are dry contacts. They work like switches for the load circuit you connect.

Question 10. Can one remote control two KR2402A receivers in Momentary mode and Toggle mode?

Answer:

Yes, it is possible, but we do not recommend it if the two receivers are close to each other.

One remote sends the same RF signal. If both receivers are in range, both will receive the signal and operate. Keep the two receivers far apart so they are not in the same range. Or use separate remotes.

Question 11. Can I add a signal amplifier to KR2402A to reach 100 meters?

Answer:

No. We do not recommend adding a signal amplifier to KR2402A.

KR2402A uses 433.92MHz remote control. To improve distance, fully extend the receiver antenna. Keep it away from metal and motors. If you need about 100 meters, use a longer-range remote with an antenna. Do not change the receiver.

docs.title: KR1201A
docs.text:
Question 01. Does KR1201A support momentary mode?

Answer:

Yes. KR1201A supports momentary mode. Hold the remote button, the relay turns on. Release the button, the relay turns off.

KR1201A also supports self-locking mode and interlocking mode. Choose the mode for your use.

Question 02. In momentary mode, what happens if the remote moves out of range while I hold the button?

Answer:

The relay will turn off. Moving out of range is the same as releasing the button. The receiver no longer gets the signal.

Question 03. Which mode should I use to reduce battery consumption?

Answer:

The receiver uses a small amount of power in standby. Momentary mode can reduce the active working time. This may help extend battery life.

For longer operating time, use a large-capacity battery or a stable 12V DC power supply.

Question 04. Will KR1201A lose the paired remotes or working mode after power loss?

Answer:

No. The relay cannot stay on when the power is off. KR1201A can remember the paired remotes and working mode after power loss.

After power comes back, the receiver will work with the saved remotes and mode. Reset will delete the saved data.

Question 05. Does toggle mode need to be set again after power loss?

Answer:

No. KR1201A will remember toggle mode after power loss. You do not need to pair or set it again after power is restored.

Question 06. Can one KR1201A receiver work with more than one remote?

Answer:

Yes. One KR1201A receiver can work with multiple remotes. Pair the first remote, then pair each new remote the same way.

Each paired remote can control the same receiver.

Question 07. Can two remotes use the same working mode, such as delay mode or toggle mode?

Answer:

Yes. Multiple remotes can pair with the same receiver and use the same working mode.

Question 08. Can KR1201A reverse a DC motor?

Answer:

No. KR1201A is a single-channel relay receiver. It can do simple on / off control. It cannot change the motor direction.

To reverse a DC motor, you need to swap the positive and negative wires. This needs a two-channel relay or a motor controller. KR1201A cannot do this.

Question 09. Can button B reverse a motor?

Answer:

No. KR1201A is a single-channel relay receiver. It cannot do forward and reverse motor control.

For motor forward and reverse control, use a two-channel controller.

Question 10. Can KR1201A control a servo motor?

Answer:

No. KR1201A is not made for servo motor control. Servo motors need a signal-based servo controller.

If you need motor forward and reverse control, use a motor controller.

Question 11. Can two KR1201A receivers control a DC motor left and right?

Answer:

No. KR1201A is not the right choice for DC motor direction control. Use a two-channel receiver or a motor controller.

Question 12. Can KR1201A be powered by AC power?

Answer:

No. KR1201A is a DC receiver. Do not connect it to AC power.

Use a DC power supply for KR1201A. If you need AC 110V or AC 220V control, choose a receiver made for that AC voltage.

Question 13. Can KR1201A control AC 220V home appliances?

Answer:

No. KR1201A is a 12V DC receiver. Do not power it with AC 220V.

If you need to control AC 220V appliances, use a relay module made for AC 220V.

Question 14. Can KR1201A and the load use the same power supply?

Answer:

Yes, but only if the voltage and current match both the receiver and the load.

Check that:

  • The receiver input voltage matches the power supply.
  • The load voltage matches the same power supply.
  • The power supply can provide enough current for both.

Wrong voltage or not enough current can damage the device.

Question 15. Can a 5V version work with a 3.7V Li-ion / 18650 battery?

Answer:

No. A 3.7V battery is lower than 5V. It may cause unstable work, shorter range, or the receiver may not work at all.

Use a proper 5V power source for a 5V receiver.

Question 16. What battery should I use for KR1201A?

Answer:

Use a 12V power source for KR1201A. A 12V lithium battery is better than AA batteries.

AA batteries may work, but they have lower capacity and may run out fast. If the device stops working, check the battery first.

Question 17. Can a 9V battery power KR1201A?

Answer:

No. KR1201A needs 12V DC. A 9V battery may light the indicator, but it cannot power the relay.

Use a proper 12V power source.

Question 18. Can two devices be connected using NO and NC?

Answer:

Yes. NO is normally open. NC is normally closed.

Connect one load through NO and another load through NC. When the relay changes, one load turns on and the other turns off. Avoid short circuits.

Question 19. Can the negative line be connected to COM?

Answer:

Many wiring diagrams connect the positive line to COM. The negative line can also connect to COM in some DC circuits, but only if you understand relay wiring.

The key rule: the circuit must not create a short circuit.

Question 20. Can KR1201A control two LED lights so that one is on while the other is off?

Answer:

Yes. Connect one LED through NO and the other LED through NC. When the relay changes, one LED turns on and the other turns off.

For more complex control, use a multi-channel relay receiver.

Question 21. What is the maximum remote-control distance?

Answer:

The actual distance depends on the remote model, antenna, environment, and interference.

Some remotes claim 2 miles or 3 km. Real-world distance is much lower, around 1 km or less.

Question 22. Can I connect an external antenna to KR1201A?

Answer:

Yes, but the new antenna must match the module specs. KR1201A has an antenna on the module. You can replace it for testing.

A wrong antenna may not improve the distance. The module may also work poorly.

Question 23. How do I reset KR1201A?

Answer:

Press the learning button 8 times to reset.

After reset, all paired remotes and mode settings will be deleted. Pair the remotes and set the mode again.

Question 24. Does KR1201A have memory storage, such as EEPROM?

Answer:

Yes. KR1201A has memory for paired remotes and mode settings.

The module can store up to 20 remotes.

Question 25. Can KR1201A be used for access control or automatic residential gates?

Answer:

Yes, for some access-control uses. KR1201A can provide dry contact control.

For an automatic residential gate, it depends on the gate controller input and voltage. For AC 220V systems or high-power uses, choose a receiver made for that system.

docs.text:
• 1: Fan speed Low/Med/High
  • 2: Switch light on/off

  • 4: Indicator light

docs.title: Wiring Diagram
docs.text: Disconnect power before wiring.

docs.title: Figure 1
docs.text:
Figure 1: Wiring diagram for AC ceiling fan light

  • Load: AC ceiling fan light
  • Input Power: AC 90V-175V

docs.title: Function description and setting method
docs.text:
(1) Pairing; (2) Unpairing; (3) Disable Buzzer; (4) Enable Buzzer.

NOTE

  • This product is intended for use only with AC motor ceiling fans and fan-lights; it is not compatible with DC motor fans or fan-lights.
  • After successful pairing, the receiver stays linked to the transmitter even if power is lost and then restored.
  • The following operations must be performed using QIACHIP-brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receivers); compatibility with products of other brands cannot be guaranteed.
  • Capacitors for our product: T4+5 µF (starting 4 µF/running 5 µF).

If the user's existing running capacitor is also 5 µF, it is the most compatible.

If the deviation is 1 µF: Nearly perfect.

If the deviation is 2 µF: A speed difference of 1-2 steps will occur.

If the deviation is 3 µF or more: Significant speed variation occurs

docs.title: (1) Pairing
docs.text:
After completing this operation:

  • Can use the remote control to turn the fan or fan-with-light on or off, and switch freely between any of the three fan speed levels.
  • Can set a timed shutdown for 1H/2H/4H/8H. (This timer function applies only to the fan, not the light.)

docs.title: How to Pair
docs.text:
Step 1

Power off the receiver, then power it back on (restart).

Step 2 Within 5 seconds of powering on the receiver, simultaneously press and hold the remote control "LIGHT ON/OFF" and "HI" buttons until the controller beeps three times, which indicates successful pairing.

docs.title: (2) Unpairing
docs.text:
After completing this operation:

  • FLC05-E110V receiver is unpaired, all previously paired transmitters lose control of it.

docs.title: How to Unpair
docs.text:
Step 1

Power off the receiver, then power it back on (restart).

Step 2

Within 5 seconds of powering on the receiver, simultaneously press and hold the remote control "STOP" and "2H" buttons until the controller beeps once times, which indicates the completion of the unpairing.

docs.title: (3) Disable Buzzer
docs.text:
After completing this operation:

  • The buzzer is turned off, and when the remote control button is pressed, the receiver will no longer emit a beep.

docs.title: How to Disable Buzzer
docs.text:
Step 1

Power off the receiver, then power it back on (restart).

Step 2

Within 5 seconds of powering on the receiver, press and hold the remote control "STOP" button until the controller beeps three times, at which point the buzzer will be turned off.

docs.title: (4) Enable Buzzer
docs.text:
After completing this operation:

docs.title: Pairing with Ewelink APP
docs.text:
Step 1

Connect your phone to 2.4GHz WIFI and turn on Bluetooth.

Step 2

Long press the learning button on the receiver for more than 6 seconds until the indicator light flashes twice quickly and then stays on once, and the WIFI pairing mode will be successfully activated.

Step 3

Open the Ewelink APP. Tap "+" to add a device in the top right corner of the screen, and enter the password of the connected 2.4G WIFI.

Step 4

Tap the icon of the device that appears to start the pairing process, and then wait until the pairing is completed.

docs.title: Compatible Pairing Mode
docs.text:
If you fail to enter Quick Pairing Mode (Touch), please try "Compatible Pairing Mode" to pair.

Step 1

Long press the learning button on the receiver for more than 5 seconds until the LED indicator flashes twice rapidly and then remains on steadily. Then release the button. Long press the pairing button for 5 seconds again until the indicator flashes rapidly. Subsequently, the device enters the compatible pairing mode.

Step 2

Tap "+" and select "Compatible Pairing Mode" on the APP. Select Wi-Fi SSID with ITEAD-** and enter the password 12345678, and then go back to the eWeLink APP and tap "Next". Be patient until pairing completes.

docs.title: Offline Pairing
docs.text:
Step 1

Press the learning button on the receiver twice in quick succession.

Step 2

The pairing can be completed by pressing the button of the remote control to be paired again.

To delete the paired remote control buttons, you need to go to the "eWelink Remote" page in the App settings for deletion.

docs.title: Pairing within APP
docs.text:
Step 1

Click on the added device, then click the settings icon in the upper - right corner, and then select "eWelink Remote".

Step 2

Simply operate according to the prompts.

docs.text:
(1) Momentary mode; (2) Toggle mode; (3) Latching mode; (4) Delay mode; (5) Reset function.

  • When you use the first and second working modes, a remote control with at least two buttons is required.
  • When you use the third working mode, a remote control with at least three buttons is required.
  • When pairing a second remote, you don't need to press the button on the receiver 8 times again to reset it.
  • Once the receiver and transmitter are paired and a working mode is selected, the receiver will retain this mode even if powered off and on again.
  • The following working modes require the use of QIACHIP brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receivers/wireless remote control switches). Compatibility with other brands is not guaranteed

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press and hold the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver is turned on.
  • Release the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver once. The indicator light on the receiver turns on and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off.The momentary mode is set successfully.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as A) again, and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver twice. The indicator light on the receiver turns on, and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off.The toggle mode is set successfully.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the receiver's relay turns on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as B), and the receiver's relay turns off.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver three times. The indicator light on the receiver turns on, and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns on.

Step 3

Within this period, press another button (such as B) on the same remote control. The indicator light on the receiver flashes and then turns off. The latching mode is set successfully.

docs.title: (4) Delay mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay of the receiver will enter delay mode.

docs.title: How to set delay mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver 4 times. The indicator light on the receiver turns on, and the receiver enters the setting state.

(Press the receiver button 4 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 5-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 5 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 10-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 6 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 15-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 7 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 20-second delay).

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off. The delay mode is set successfully,

docs.title: (5) Reset function
docs.text: • When the KR1201A receiver is reset, all paired transmitters will be unpaired and can no longer control the receiver.

docs.title: How to Reset
docs.text: Click the learning button on the receiver 8 times. The reset is complete when the indicator light flashes and then turns off.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions（Q&A）
docs.text:
Q1: Does KR1201A support momentary mode?

A: Yes. KR1201A supports momentary mode. Hold the remote button, the relay turns on. Release the button, the relay turns off.

KR1201A also supports self-locking mode and interlocking mode. Choose the mode for your use.

Q2: In momentary mode, what happens if the remote moves out of range while I hold the button?

A: The relay will turn off. Moving out of range is the same as releasing the button. The receiver no longer gets the signal.

Q3: Which mode should I use to reduce battery consumption?

A: The receiver uses a small amount of power in standby. Momentary mode can reduce the active working time. This may help extend battery life.

For longer operating time, use a large-capacity battery or a stable 12V DC power supply.

Q4: Will KR1201A lose the paired remotes or working mode after power loss?

A: No. The relay cannot stay on when the power is off. KR1201A can remember the paired remotes and working mode after power loss.

After power comes back, the receiver will work with the saved remotes and mode. Reset will delete the saved data.

Q5: Does toggle mode need to be set again after power loss?

A: No. KR1201A will remember toggle mode after power loss. You do not need to pair or set it again after power is restored.

Q6: Can one KR1201A receiver work with more than one remote?

A: Yes. One KR1201A receiver can work with multiple remotes. Pair the first remote, then pair each new remote the same way.

Each paired remote can control the same receiver.

Q7: Can two remotes use the same working mode, such as delay mode or toggle mode?

A: Yes. Multiple remotes can pair with the same receiver and use the same working mode.

Q8: Can KR1201A reverse a DC motor?

A: No. KR1201A is a single-channel relay receiver. It can do simple on / off control. It cannot change the motor direction.

To reverse a DC motor, you need to swap the positive and negative wires. This needs a two-channel relay or a motor controller. KR1201A cannot do this.

Q9: Can button B reverse a motor?

A: No. KR1201A is a single-channel relay receiver. It cannot do forward and reverse motor control.

For motor forward and reverse control, use a two-channel controller.

Q10: Can KR1201A control a servo motor?

A: No. KR1201A is not made for servo motor control. Servo motors need a signal-based servo controller.

If you need motor forward and reverse control, use a motor controller.

Q11: Can two KR1201A receivers control a DC motor left and right?

A: No. KR1201A is not the right choice for DC motor direction control. Use a two-channel receiver or a motor controller.

Q12: Can KR1201A be powered by AC power?

A: No. KR1201A is a DC receiver. Do not connect it to AC power.

Use a DC power supply for KR1201A. If you need AC 110V or AC 220V control, choose a receiver made for that AC voltage.

Q13: Can KR1201A control AC 220V home appliances?

A: No. KR1201A is a 12V DC receiver. Do not power it with AC 220V.

If you need to control AC 220V appliances, use a relay module made for AC 220V.

Q14: Can KR1201A and the load use the same power supply?

A: Yes, but only if the voltage and current match both the receiver and the load.

Check that:

  • The receiver input voltage matches the power supply.
  • The load voltage matches the same power supply.
  • The power supply can provide enough current for both.

Wrong voltage or not enough current can damage the device.

Q15: Can a 5V version work with a 3.7V Li-ion / 18650 battery?

A: No. A 3.7V battery is lower than 5V. It may cause unstable work, shorter range, or the receiver may not work at all.

Use a proper 5V power source for a 5V receiver.

Q16: What battery should I use for KR1201A?

A: Use a 12V power source for KR1201A. A 12V lithium battery is better than AA batteries.

AA batteries may work, but they have lower capacity and may run out fast. If the device stops working, check the battery first.

Q17: Can a 9V battery power KR1201A?

A: No. KR1201A needs 12V DC. A 9V battery may light the indicator, but it cannot power the relay.

Use a proper 12V power source.

Q18: Can two devices be connected using NO and NC?

A: Yes. NO is normally open. NC is normally closed.

Connect one load through NO and another load through NC. When the relay changes, one load turns on and the other turns off. Avoid short circuits.

Q19: Can the negative line be connected to COM?

A: Many wiring diagrams connect the positive line to COM. The negative line can also connect to COM in some DC circuits, but only if you understand relay wiring.

The key rule: the circuit must not create a short circuit.

Q20: Can KR1201A control two LED lights so that one is on while the other is off?

A: Yes. Connect one LED through NO and the other LED through NC. When the relay changes, one LED turns on and the other turns off.

For more complex control, use a multi-channel relay receiver.

Q21: What is the maximum remote-control distance?

A: The actual distance depends on the remote model, antenna, environment, and interference.

Some remotes claim 2 miles or 3 km. Real-world distance is much lower, around 1 km or less.

Q22: Can I connect an external antenna to KR1201A?

A: Yes, but the new antenna must match the module specs. KR1201A has an antenna on the module. You can replace it for testing.

A wrong antenna may not improve the distance. The module may also work poorly.

Q23: How do I reset KR1201A?

A: Press the learning button 8 times to reset.

After reset, all paired remotes and mode settings will be deleted. Pair the remotes and set the mode again.

Q24: Does KR1201A have memory storage, such as EEPROM?

A: Yes. KR1201A has memory for paired remotes and mode settings.

The module can store up to 20 remotes.

Q25: Can KR1201A be used for access control or automatic residential gates?

A: Yes, for some access-control uses. KR1201A can provide dry contact control.

For an automatic residential gate, it depends on the gate controller input and voltage. For AC 220V systems or high-power uses, choose a receiver made for that system.

docs.title: Function description and setting method
docs.text:
(1) Momentary mode; (2) Toggle mode; (3) Latching mode; (4) Delay mode; (5) Reset function.

NOTE

  • When you use the third working mode, a remote control with at least two buttons is required.
  • When pairing a second remote, you don't need to press the button on the receiver 8 times again to reset it.
  • Once the receiver and transmitter are paired and a working mode is selected, the receiver will retain this mode even if powered off and on again.
  • The following working modes require the use of QIACHIP brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receivers/wireless remote control switches). Compatibility with other brands is not guaranteed.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press and hold the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Release the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver once. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The momentary mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as A) again, and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver twice. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The toggle mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the receiver's relay will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as B), and the receiver's relay will turn off.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver three times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn on.

Step 3

After the indicator light turns on, press another button (such as B) on the same remote control. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off. The latching mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (4) Delay mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay of the receiver will enter delay mode.

docs.title: How to set delay mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver 4 times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

(Press the receiver button 4 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 5-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 5 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 10-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 6 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 15-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 7 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 20-second delay).

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off. The delay mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (5) Reset function
docs.text: • When the KR1201C receiver is reset, all paired transmitters will be unpaired and will no longer be able to control the receiver.

docs.title: How to reset
docs.text: Click the learning button on the receiver 8 times. The indicator light will flash and then will turn off. The reset will be complete.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode

docs.title: (5) 2CH Momentary mode + 2CH Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

The Momentary mode channels (Channel 1, Channel 2) and Toggle mode channels (Channel 3, Channel 4) operate independently. Activating or deactivating a relay in one mode has no impact on the state of relays in the other mode

For the two channels set to Momentary mode (Channel 1 and Channel 2):

  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 1 (such as A), and relay channel 1 on the receiver will turn on; release button A, and relay channel 1 will turn off.
  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 2 (such as B), and relay channel 2 on the receiver will turn on; release button B, and relay channel 2 will turn off.

For the two channels set to Toggle mode (Channel 3 and Channel 4):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 3 (such as C), and relay channel 3 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button C again, and relay channel 3 will turn off.
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 4 (such as D), and relay channel 4 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button D again, and relay channel 4 will turn off.

docs.title: How to set 2CH Momentary mode + 2CH Toggle mode mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver four times. The indicator light on the receiver turns on, and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off. The 2CH momentary mode + 2CH toggle mode is set successfully.

docs.title: (6) 2CH Momentary mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

The Momentary mode channels (Channel 1, Channel 2) and Latching mode channels (Channel 3, Channel 4) operate independently. Activating or deactivating a relay in one mode has no impact on the state of relays in the other mode

For the two channels set to Momentary mode (Channel 1 and Channel 2):

  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 1 (such as A), and relay channel 1 on the receiver will turn on; release button A, and relay channel 1 will turn off.
  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 2 (such as B), and relay channel 2 on the receiver will turn on; release button B, and relay channel 2 will turn off.

For the two channels set to Latching mode (Channel 3 and Channel 4):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 3 (such as C), and relay channel 3 on the receiver will turn on;
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 4 (such as D), and relay channel 4 on the receiver will turn on, while relay channel 3 turns off simultaneously.

docs.title: How to set 2CH Momentary mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver five times. The indicator light on the receiver turns on, and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off. The 2CH momentary mode + 2CH latching mode is set successfully.

docs.title: (7) 2CH Toggle mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

The Toggle mode channels (Channel 1, Channel 2) and Latching mode channels (Channel 3, Channel 4) operate independently. Activating or deactivating a relay in one mode has no impact on the state of relays in the other mode

For the two channels set to Toggle mode (Channel 1 and Channel 2):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 1 (such as A), and relay channel 1 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button A again, and relay channel 1 will turn off.
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 2 (such as B), and relay channel 2 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button B again, and relay channel 2 will turn off.

For the two channels set to Latching mode (Channel 3 and Channel 4):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 3 (such as C), and relay channel 3 on the receiver will turn on;
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 4 (such as D), and relay channel 4 on the receiver will turn on, while relay channel 3 turns off simultaneously.

docs.title: How to set 2CH Toggle mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver six times. The indicator light on the receiver turns on, and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off. The 2CH toggle mode + 2CH latching mode is set successfully.

docs.title: (8) 2CH Latching mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

The Latching mode channels (Channel 1, Channel 2) and Latching mode channels (Channel 3, Channel 4) operate independently. Activating or deactivating a relay in one mode has no impact on the state of relays in the other mode

For the two channels set to Latching mode (Channel 1 and Channel 2):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 1 (such as A), and relay channel 1 on the receiver will turn on;
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 2 (such as B), and relay channel 2 on the receiver will turn on, while relay channel 1 turns off simultaneously.

For the two channels set to Latching mode (Channel 3 and Channel 4):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 3 (such as C), and relay channel 3 on the receiver will turn on;
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 4 (such as D), and relay channel 4 on the receiver will turn on, while relay channel 3 turns off simultaneously.

docs.title: How to set 2CH Latching mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver seven times. The indicator light on the receiver turns on, and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off. The 2CH latching mode + 2CH latching mode is set successfully.

docs.title: NOTE
docs.text:
• When using the KR1204 model receiver, at least one remote control with four buttons is required.
  • When pairing a second remote, you don't need to press the button on the receiver 8 times again to reset it.
  • Once the receiver and transmitter are paired and a working mode is selected, the receiver will retain this mode even if powered off and on again.
  • The following working modes require the use of QIACHIP brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receivers/wireless remote control switches). Compatibility with other brands is not guaranteed.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press and hold the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Release the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button on the receiver once. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The momentary mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as A) again, and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button on the receiver twice. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The toggle mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay A on the receiver will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as B), and the corresponding relay B on the receiver will turn on while relay A on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver three times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off. The latching mode is set successfully.

docs.title: (4) Reset function
docs.text: • When the KR1204B receiver is reset, all paired transmitters will be unpaired and will no longer be able to control the receiver.

docs.title: How to reset
docs.text: Click the learning button on the receiver 8 times. The reset is complete when the indicator light flashes and then turns off.

docs.title: (5) 2CH Momentary mode + 2CH Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

The Momentary mode channels (Channel 1, Channel 2) and Toggle mode channels (Channel 3, Channel 4) operate independently. Activating or deactivating a relay in one mode has no impact on the state of relays in the other mode.

For the two channels set to Momentary mode (Channel 1 and Channel 2):

  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 1 (such as A), and relay channel 1 on the receiver will turn on; release button A, and relay channel 1 will turn off.
  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 2 (such as B), and relay channel 2 on the receiver will turn on; release button B, and relay channel 2 will turn off.

For the two channels set to Toggle mode (Channel 3 and Channel 4):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 3 (such as C), and relay channel 3 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button C again, and relay channel 3 will turn off.
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 4 (such as D), and relay channel 4 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button D again, and relay channel 4 will turn off.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode

In this mode:

docs.title: Pairing with Ewelink APP
docs.text:
Step 1

Connect your phone to 2.4GHz WIFI and turn on Bluetooth.

Step 2

Long press the learning button on the receiver for more than 6 seconds until the indicator light flashes twice quickly and then stays on once, and the WIFI pairing mode will be successfully activated.

Step 3

Open the Ewelink APP. Tap "+" to add a device in the top right corner of the screen, and enter the password of the connected 2.4G WIFI.

Step 4

Tap the icon of the device that appears to start the pairing process, and then wait until the pairing is completed.

docs.title: Compatible Pairing Mode
docs.text:
If you fail to enter Quick Pairing Mode (Touch), please try "Compatible Pairing Mode" to pair.

Step 1

Long press the learning button on the receiver for more than 5 seconds until the LED indicator flashes twice rapidly and then remains on steadily. Then release the button. Long press the pairing button for 5 seconds again until the indicator flashes rapidly. Subsequently, the device enters the compatible pairing mode.

Step 2

Tap "+" and select "Compatible Pairing Mode" on APP. Select Wi-Fi SSID with ITEAD-** and enter the password 12345678, and then go back to eWeLink APP and tap "Next". Be patient until pairing completes.

docs.title: Offline Pairing
docs.text:
Step 1

Press the learning button on the receiver twice in quick succession.

Step 2

The pairing can be completed by pressing the button of the remote control to be paired again.

To delete the paired remote control buttons, you need to go to the "eWelink Remote" page in the App settings for deletion.

docs.title: Pairing within APP
docs.text:
Step 1

Click on the added device, then click the settings icon in the upper - right corner, and then select "eWelink Remote".

Step 2

Simply operate according to the prompts.

  • When you use the third working mode, a remote control with at least two buttons is required.
  • When pairing a second remote, you don't need to press the button on the receiver 8 times again to reset it.
  • Once the receiver and transmitter are paired and a working mode is selected, the receiver will retain this mode even if powered off and on again.
  • The following working modes require the use of QIACHIP brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receivers/wireless remote control switches). Compatibility with other brands is not guaranteed.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press and hold the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Release the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver four times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The momentary mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as A) again, and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver twice. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off (Pairing successful). The toggle mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the receiver's relay will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as B), and the receiver's relay will turn off.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver three times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn on (Setting in progress).

Step 3

After the indicator light turns on, press another button (such as B) on the same remote control. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off (Pairing successful). The latching mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (4) Reset function
docs.text: • When the KR2201W-4 receiver is reset, all paired transmitters will be unpaired and will no longer be able to control the receiver.

docs.title: How to reset
docs.text: Click the learning button on the receiver 8 times. The indicator light will flash and then will turn off. The reset will be complete.

docs.title: (5) Power-On Auto-Engagement
docs.text: • After this mode is set, the relay will automatically turn on when the receiver is powered back on after a power outage.

docs.title: How to set power-on auto-engagement
docs.text: Press the receiver's learning button 6 times in quick succession until the indicator light flashes and then goes off (Setting successful). (After successful setup, the relay will remain in the energized state when the device is powered back on, regardless of its state before the power outage.)

docs.title: Pairing with Tuya APP
docs.text:
Step 1

Connect your phone to 2.4GHz WIFI and turn on Bluetooth.

Step 2

Long press the learning button on the receiver for more than 6 seconds until the indicator light flashes, and the WIFI pairing mode will be successfully activated.

Step 3

Open the Tuya APP. Tap "+" to add a device in the top right corner of the screen, and then tap the device that appears to start the pairing process.

Step 4

Enter the password of the connected 2.4G WIFI, and then wait until the device pairing is completed.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions（Q&A）
docs.text:
Question 1: Why can my remote control Relay 1 but not Relay 2?

Answer:

Your setup is wrong, so your remote can only control Relay 1. The receiver is not broken.

You need to pair it again with the steps below.

  1. Press the Learning button 8 times to reset.
  2. Press the Learning button 1 time for Momentary mode.
  3. Press the button for Relay 1. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  4. Press the button for Relay 2. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

This sets both relays.

Question 2: Why do both button A and button B control Relay 1 only after Momentary pairing?

Answer:

Your pairing steps are wrong, so button A and button B both control Relay 1 only. The receiver is not broken.

You need to pair it again with the steps below.

  1. Press the Learning button 8 times to reset.
  2. Press the Learning button 1 time for Momentary mode.
  3. Press button A. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  4. Press button B. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

This sets both relays.

Question 3: Momentary, Toggle, and Latching mode. What is the difference?

Answer:

KR2202 has three working modes.

  • Momentary mode: hold the button, the relay turns on. Release the button, the relay turns off.
  • Toggle mode: press the button once, the relay turns on. Press the same button again, the relay turns off.
  • Latching mode: press button A, Relay 1 turns on. Press button B, Relay 2 turns on and Relay 1 turns off. Press the STOP button, the active relay turns off.

Use Momentary mode for hold-to-run control. Use Toggle mode for on / off control. Use Latching mode when two directions must not turn on at the same time.

Question 4: Do the two relays trigger at the same time or one by one?

Answer:

No. KR2202 does not have an auto-sequence mode.

The two relays are controlled by different remote buttons. In Momentary mode or Toggle mode, button A controls Relay 1 and button B controls Relay 2.

Question 5: How do I set two remotes to control Relay 1 and Relay 2 on KR2202?

Answer:

Pair one button on the first remote to Relay 1. Pair one button on the second remote to Relay 2. Do both in the same setup.

Example for Toggle mode:

  1. Press the Learning button on the receiver 2 times. Wait until the light turns off.
  2. Press the button on the first remote for Relay 1. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  3. Press the button on the second remote for Relay 2. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

If the pairing is wrong, press the Learning button 8 times to reset. Reset will delete all paired remotes.

Question 6: Can I pair multiple remotes to channels A and B in Momentary mode?

Answer:

Yes. KR2202 can pair multiple remotes to channels A and B in Momentary mode.

For each new remote, pair both buttons in the same Momentary setup. Do not pair button A alone.

Press the Learning button 8 times only if you want to reset. Reset will delete all paired remotes.

Question 7: Why does my 3-button UP / STOP / DOWN remote not work for motor control in Toggle mode?

Answer:

Toggle mode is not the right mode for UP / STOP / DOWN motor control. Use Latching mode instead.

Pair UP and DOWN in the same Latching mode setup:

  1. Press the Learning button 8 times to reset.
  2. Press the Learning button 3 times to enter Latching mode.
  3. Press UP. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  4. Press DOWN. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

After this setup: - Press UP, the motor runs up. - Press DOWN, the motor runs down and UP turns off. - Press STOP, the active relay turns off.

For motor control, the up and down directions must not turn on at the same time.

Question 8: How do I use KR2202 Momentary mode to control a two-direction motor with button A and button B?

Answer:

Yes. You can use KR2202 Momentary mode for a two-direction motor.

  1. Press the Learning button 1 time. Wait until the light turns off.
  2. Press button A on the remote. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  3. Press button B on the remote. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

In Momentary mode, the relay turns on while you hold the button. For a two-direction motor, both directions must not turn on at the same time.

If the motor system has its own control input, connect KR2202 to that input. Do not connect KR2202 directly to the motor power wires.

Question 9: Does the KR2202 relay stay on after power loss?

Answer:

No. The relay cannot stay on when the power is off.

KR2202 can remember the paired remote and working mode after power loss. But the relay needs power to stay on. After power comes back, the next relay action depends on the mode and remote command.

Question 10: Is the KR2202 receiver powered by 220V, or are the relay contacts rated for 220V?

Answer:

Yes, the KR2202 receiver can be powered by 220V AC. But the relay contacts do not output 220V by themselves.

The relay output is a dry contact. This means it works like a switch. It does not give power by itself. The terminals are NO, COM, and NC.

Question 11: Is KR2202 compatible with 230V AC?

Answer:

Yes. KR2202 can work with 230V AC power.

Its input voltage range is AC 85V-250V, so 230V AC is within the supported range.

Question 12: How should I wire KR2202 for my load?

Answer:

It depends on what load you want to control.

Use the wiring diagram that matches your load, such as a motor, lamp, or other device. Turn off power before wiring. Do not reverse L and N. Keep the load within Max 1100W.

L and N power the KR2202 receiver itself. The relay terminals do not output power by themselves. They are dry contacts. They work like switches for the load circuit you connect.

Question 13: Can one KR2202 remote control two garage door motors?

Answer:

It depends on what you mean by garage door motor.

If you mean a garage door opener with a control input, yes. KR2202 can trigger the control input. It works like pressing a wall button.

Do not use KR2202 to drive bare garage door motors directly. KR2202 is a relay receiver, not a motor controller.

Question 14: Can KR2202 control a winch raise and lower motor?

Answer:

Yes, but only if the winch motor matches KR2202.

Check the motor voltage, running current, and starting current first. KR2202 input is AC 85V-250V. The rated load is Max 1100W. For raise and lower control, the up and down directions must not turn on at the same time.

Question 15: Is KR2202 suitable for a roller shutter or garage shutter motor?

Answer:

Yes. KR2202 can work for some roller shutter or garage shutter motors.

Key points:

  • The shutter motor or controller must match KR2202 power and load limits: AC 85V-250V input and Max 1100W load.
  • Momentary mode is a good choice. The motor runs while you hold the button.
  • If the shutter has a manual wall switch, connect KR2202 COM and NO across the switch terminals. This adds remote control.

Question 16: Can KR2202 use button A and button B for gate open and close in Momentary mode?

Answer:

Yes, if your gate controller has dry contact OPEN and CLOSE inputs.

Use Relay 1 for OPEN and Relay 2 for CLOSE. Set KR2202 to Momentary mode. The gate will respond while you hold the button. Connect KR2202 only to dry contact input terminals.

Question 17: How do I identify a 3-wire roller shutter motor? Does KR2202 stop at the end position?

Answer:

No. Do not assume the third wire is ground. KR2202 does not stop the shutter at the end position by itself.

A 3-wire shutter motor may be Live / Neutral / Ground, or it may be Common / Up / Down. Check the motor label or manual before wiring. KR2202 turns the relay on and off. The shutter motor or controller must have its own limit switch.

Question 18: Why does KR2202 work with no load but fail when I connect a pool cover motor?

Answer:

KR2202 is probably not the broken part. The problem is the motor load or the motor control method.

The two most likely reasons are:

  1. The motor starting current is too high. A motor needs more current when it starts than when it runs. Even if the running power is under Max 1100W, the starting current may be too high for KR2202. Do not use KR2202 to switch the motor power directly. Use a motor controller or contactor. Or check the starting current before wiring.

  2. The pool cover motor may need its own control input. KR2202 is a relay receiver, not a motor controller. It works like a switch through NO and COM. If the pool cover system has a wall switch or control input, connect KR2202 to that input. Do not switch the motor power wires directly.

Check the motor label or manual for starting current and control input type. If you are not sure, ask an electrician before wiring.

Question 19: Can one remote control two KR2202 receivers in Momentary mode and Toggle mode?

Answer:

Yes, it is possible, but we do not recommend it if the two receivers are close to each other.

One remote sends the same RF signal. If both receivers are in range, both will receive the signal and operate. Keep the two receivers far apart so they are not in the same range. Or use separate remotes.

Question 20: Can I add a signal amplifier to KR2202 to reach 100 meters?

Answer:

No. We do not recommend adding a signal amplifier to KR2202.

KR2202 uses 433.92MHz remote control. To improve distance, fully extend the receiver antenna. Keep it away from metal and motors. If you need about 100 meters, use a longer-range remote with an antenna. Do not change the receiver.

docs.title: (5) 2CH Momentary mode + 2CH Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

The Momentary mode channels (Channel 1, Channel 2) and Toggle mode channels (Channel 3, Channel 4) operate independently. Activating or deactivating a relay in one mode has no impact on the state of relays in the other mode.

For the two channels set to Momentary mode (Channel 1 and Channel 2):

  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 1 (such as A), and relay channel 1 on the receiver will turn on; release button A, and relay channel 1 will turn off.
  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 2 (such as B), and relay channel 2 on the receiver will turn on; release button B, and relay channel 2 will turn off.

For the two channels set to Toggle mode (Channel 3 and Channel 4):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 3 (such as C), and relay channel 3 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button C again, and relay channel 3 will turn off.
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 4 (such as D), and relay channel 4 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button D again, and relay channel 4 will turn off.

docs.title: How to set 2CH Momentary mode + 2CH Toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver four times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The 2CH momentary mode + 2CH toggle mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (6) 2CH Momentary mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

The Momentary mode channels (Channel 1, Channel 2) and Latching mode channels (Channel 3, Channel 4) operate independently. Activating or deactivating a relay in one mode has no impact on the state of relays in the other mode.

For the two channels set to Momentary mode (Channel 1 and Channel 2):

  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 1 (such as A), and relay channel 1 on the receiver will turn on; release button A, and relay channel 1 will turn off.
  • Press and hold the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 2 (such as B), and relay channel 2 on the receiver will turn on; release button B, and relay channel 2 will turn off.

For the two channels set to Latching mode (Channel 3 and Channel 4):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 3 (such as C), and relay channel 3 on the receiver will turn on;
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 4 (such as D), and relay channel 4 on the receiver will turn on, while relay channel 3 turns off simultaneously.

docs.title: How to set 2CH Momentary mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver five times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The 2CH momentary mode + 2CH latching mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (7) 2CH Toggle mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

The Toggle mode channels (Channel 1, Channel 2) and Latching mode channels (Channel 3, Channel 4) operate independently. Activating or deactivating a relay in one mode has no impact on the state of relays in the other mode.

For the two channels set to Toggle mode (Channel 1 and Channel 2):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 1 (such as A), and relay channel 1 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button A again, and relay channel 1 will turn off.
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 2 (such as B), and relay channel 2 on the receiver will turn on; Press the remote control button B again, and relay channel 2 will turn off.

For the two channels set to Latching mode (Channel 3 and Channel 4):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 3 (such as C), and relay channel 3 on the receiver will turn on;
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 4 (such as D), and relay channel 4 on the receiver will turn on, while relay channel 3 turns off simultaneously.

docs.title: How to set 2CH Toggle mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver six times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The 2CH toggle mode + 2CH latching mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (8) 2CH Latching mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

The Latching mode channels (Channel 1, Channel 2) and Latching mode channels (Channel 3, Channel 4) operate independently. Activating or deactivating a relay in one mode has no impact on the state of relays in the other mode.

For the two channels set to Latching mode (Channel 1 and Channel 2):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 1 (such as A), and relay channel 1 on the receiver will turn on;
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 2 (such as B), and relay channel 2 on the receiver will turn on, while relay channel 1 turns off simultaneously.

For the two channels set to Latching mode (Channel 3 and Channel 4):

  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 3 (such as C), and relay channel 3 on the receiver will turn on;
  • Press the remote control button corresponding to relay channel 4 (such as D), and relay channel 4 on the receiver will turn on, while relay channel 3 turns off simultaneously.

docs.title: How to set 2CH Latching mode + 2CH Latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver seven times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The 2CH latching mode + 2CH latching mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: Pairing with Ewelink APP
docs.text:
Step 1

Connect your phone to 2.4GHz WIFI and turn on Bluetooth.

Step 2

Long press the learning button on the receiver for more than 6 seconds until the indicator light flashes twice quickly and then stays on once, and the WIFI pairing mode will be successfully activated.

Step 3

Open the Ewelink APP. Tap "+" to add a device in the top right corner of the screen, and enter the password of the connected 2.4G WIFI.

Step 4

Tap the icon of the device that appears to start the pairing process, and then wait until the pairing is completed.

docs.title: Compatible Pairing Mode
docs.text:
If you fail to enter Quick Pairing Mode (Touch), please try "Compatible Pairing Mode" to pair.

Step 1

Long press the learning button on the receiver for more than 5 seconds until the LED indicator flashes twice rapidly and then remains on steadily. Then release the button. Long press the pairing button for 5 seconds again until the indicator flashes rapidly. Subsequently, the device enters the compatible pairing mode.

Step 2

Tap "+" and select "Compatible Pairing Mode" on APP. Select Wi-Fi SSID with ITEAD-** and enter the password 12345678, and then go back to eWeLink APP and tap "Next". Be patient until pairing completes.

docs.title: Offline Pairing
docs.text:
Step 1

Press the learning button on the receiver twice in quick succession.

Step 2

The pairing can be completed by pressing the button of the remote control to be paired again.

To delete the paired remote control buttons, you need to go to the "eWelink Remote" page in the App settings for deletion.

docs.title: Pairing within APP
docs.text:
Step 1

Click on the added device, then click the settings icon in the upper - right corner, and then select "eWelink Remote".

Step 2

Simply operate according to the prompts.

docs.title: Function description and setting method
docs.text:
(1) Momentary mode; (2) Toggle mode; (3) Latching mode; (4) Delay mode; (5) Reset function.

  • When you use the third working mode, a remote control with at least two buttons is required.
  • When pairing a second remote, you don't need to press the button on the receiver 8 times again to reset it.
  • Once the receiver and transmitter are paired and a working mode is selected, the receiver will retain this mode even if powered off and on again.
  • The following working modes require the use of QIACHIP brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receivers). Compatibility with other brands is not guaranteed.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press and hold the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Release the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver once. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The momentary mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as A) again, and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver twice. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The toggle mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the receiver's relay will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as B), and the receiver's relay will turn off.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver three times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn on.

Step 3

After the indicator light turns on, press another button (such as B) on the same remote control. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off. The latching mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (4) Delay mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay of the receiver will enter delay mode.

docs.title: How to set delay mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver 4 times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

(Press the receiver button 4 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 5-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 5 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 10-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 6 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 15-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 7 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 20-second delay).

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off. The delay mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (5) Reset function
docs.text: • When the KR2401FB receiver is reset, all paired transmitters will be unpaired and will no longer be able to control the receiver.

docs.title: How to reset
docs.text: Click the learning button on the receiver 8 times. The indicator light will flash and then will turn off. The reset will be complete.
