(1) Momentary mode; (2) Toggle mode; (3) Latching mode; (4) Delay mode; (5) Reset function.

  • When you use to third working mode, a remote control with at least two buttons is required.
  • When pairing a second remote, you don't need to press to button on the receiver 8 times again to reset it.
  • Once the receiver and transmitter are paired and a working mode is selected, the receiver will retain this mode even if powered off and on again.
  • The following working modes require the use of QIACHIP brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receivers). Compatibility with other brands is not guaranteed.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press and hold the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Release the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver once. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The momentary mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as A) again, and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver twice. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The toggle mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the receiver's relay will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as B), and the receiver's relay will turn off.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver three times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn on.

Step 3

After the indicator light turns on, press another button (such as B) on the same remote control. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off. The latching mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (4) Delay mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay of the receiver will enter delay mode.

docs.title: How to set delay mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver 4 times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

(Press the receiver button 4 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 5-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 5 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 10-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 6 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 15-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 7 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 20-second delay).

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off. The delay mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (5) Reset function
docs.text: • When the KR2401FB receiver is reset, all paired transmitters will be unpaired and will no longer be able to control the receiver.

docs.title: How to reset
docs.text: Click the learning button on the receiver 8 times. The indicator light will flash and then will turn off. The reset will be complete.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press and hold the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver is turned on.
  • Release the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
NOTE

  • The KR2402 can only be properly set up after being successfully paired with two different buttons on the remote control.

Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver once. The indicator light on the receiver turns off and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off.

Step 3

After the indicator light goes out, press another button (such as B) on the same remote control. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn on. The momentary mode is set successfully.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as A) again, and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
NOTE

  • The KR2402 can only be properly set up after being successfully paired with two different buttons on the remote control.

Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver twice. The indicator light on the receiver turns off, and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off.

Step 3

After the indicator light goes out, press another button (such as B) on the same remote control. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn on. The toggle mode is set successfully.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding receiver’s relay A is turned on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as B), and the receiver’s relay B is turned on, and the receiver’s relay A is turned off.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
NOTE

  • The KR2402 can only be properly set up after being successfully paired with two different buttons on the remote control.

Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver three times. The indicator light on the receiver turns off, and the receiver enters the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turns off.

Step 3

After the indicator light goes out, press another button (such as B) on the same remote control. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn on. The latching mode is set successfully.

docs.title: Frequently Asked Questions（Q&A）
docs.text:
Question 1: Why can my remote control Relay 1 but not Relay 2?

Answer:

Your setup is wrong, so your remote can only control Relay 1. The receiver is not broken.

You need to pair it again with the steps below.

  1. Press the Learning button 8 times to reset.
  2. Press the Learning button 1 time for Momentary mode.
  3. Press the button for Relay 1. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  4. Press the button for Relay 2. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

This sets both relays.

Question 2: Why do both button A and button B control Relay 1 only after Momentary pairing?

Answer:

Your pairing steps are wrong, so button A and button B both control Relay 1 only. The receiver is not broken.

You need to pair it again with the steps below.

  1. Press the Learning button 8 times to reset.
  2. Press the Learning button 1 time for Momentary mode.
  3. Press button A. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  4. Press button B. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

This sets both relays.

Question 3: Momentary, Toggle, and Latching mode. What is the difference?

Answer:

KR2402A has three working modes.

  • Momentary mode: hold the button, the relay turns on. Release the button, the relay turns off.
  • Toggle mode: press the button once, the relay turns on. Press the same button again, the relay turns off.
  • Latching mode: press button A, Relay 1 turns on. Press button B, Relay 2 turns on and Relay 1 turns off.

Use Momentary mode for hold-to-run control. Use Toggle mode for on / off control. Use Latching mode when Relay 1 and Relay 2 must not stay on at the same time.

Question 4: Do the two relays trigger at the same time or one by one?

Answer:

No. KR2402A does not have an auto-sequence mode.

The two relays are controlled by different remote buttons. In Momentary mode or Toggle mode, button A controls Relay 1 and button B controls Relay 2.

Question 5: How do I set two remotes to control Relay 1 and Relay 2 on KR2402A?

Answer:

Pair one button on the first remote to Relay 1. Pair one button on the second remote to Relay 2. Do both in the same setup.

Example for Toggle mode:

  1. Press the Learning button on the receiver 2 times. Wait until the light turns off.
  2. Press the button on the first remote for Relay 1. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  3. Press the button on the second remote for Relay 2. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

If the pairing is wrong, press the Learning button 8 times to reset. Reset will delete all paired remotes.

Question 6: Can I pair multiple remotes to channels A and B in Momentary mode?

Answer:

Yes. KR2402A can pair multiple remotes to channels A and B in Momentary mode.

For each new remote, pair both buttons in the same Momentary setup. Do not pair button A alone.

Press the Learning button 8 times only if you want to reset. Reset will delete all paired remotes.

Question 7: How do I use KR2402A Momentary mode to control a two-direction motor with button A and button B?

Answer:

Yes. You can use KR2402A Momentary mode for a two-direction motor.

  1. Press the Learning button 1 time. Wait until the light turns off.
  2. Press button A on the remote. Wait until the light flashes, then turns off.
  3. Press button B on the remote. Wait until the light flashes, then turns on.

In Momentary mode, the relay turns on while you hold the button. For a two-direction motor, both directions must not turn on at the same time.

If the motor system has its own control input, connect KR2402A to that input. Do not connect KR2402A directly to the motor power wires.

Question 8: Does the KR2402A relay stay on after power loss?

Answer:

No. The relay cannot stay on when the power is off.

KR2402A can remember the paired remote and working mode after power loss. But the relay needs power to stay on. After power comes back, the next relay action depends on the mode and remote command.

Question 9: How should I wire KR2402A for my load?

Answer:

It depends on what load you want to control.

Use the wiring diagram that matches your load, such as a lamp, AC motor, or DC motor. Turn off power before wiring.

+V and -V power the KR2402A receiver itself. The receiver input is DC 5V-60V. For relay output wiring, use the correct terminals: NO1 / COM1 / NC1 for Relay 1, and NO2 / COM2 / NC2 for Relay 2.

The relay terminals do not output power by themselves. They are dry contacts. They work like switches for the load circuit you connect.

Question 10: Can one remote control two KR2402A receivers in Momentary mode and Toggle mode?

Answer:

Yes, it is possible, but we do not recommend it if the two receivers are close to each other.

One remote sends the same RF signal. If both receivers are in range, both will receive the signal and operate. Keep the two receivers far apart so they are not in the same range. Or use separate remotes.

Question 11: Can I add a signal amplifier to KR2402A to reach 100 meters?

Answer:

No. We do not recommend adding a signal amplifier to KR2402A.

KR2402A uses 433.92MHz remote control. To improve distance, fully extend the receiver antenna. Keep it away from metal and motors. If you need about 100 meters, use a longer-range remote with an antenna. Do not change the receiver.

• Enter Power Saving Mode:

Press the learn button four times. The indicator light will flash and then turn off, indicating the module has entered power saving mode.

Press the learn button five times. The indicator light will flash and then turn off, indicating the module has exited power saving mode.

docs.text:
(1) Momentary mode;

(3) Latching mode;

(4) Delay mode;

(5) Reset function;

(6) Power-On Auto-Engagement.

  • When you use the third working mode, a remote control with at least two buttons is required.
  • When pairing a second remote, you don't need to press the button on the receiver 8 times again to reset it.
  • Once the receiver and transmitter are paired and a working mode is selected, the receiver will retain this mode even if powered off and on again.
  • The following working modes require the use of QIACHIP brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receivers/wireless remote control switches). Compatibility with other brands is not guaranteed.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press and hold the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Release the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver once. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The momentary mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as A) again, and the corresponding relay on the receiver will turn off.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver twice. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn off. The toggle mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the receiver's relay will turn on.
  • Press the remote control button (such as B), and the receiver's relay will turn off.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver three times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then will turn on.

Step 3

After the indicator light turns on, press another button (such as B) on the same remote control. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off. The latching mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (4) Delay mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the remote control button (such as A), and the corresponding relay of the receiver will enter delay mode.

docs.title: How to set delay mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button of the receiver 4 times. The indicator light on the receiver will turn on, and the receiver will enter the setting state.

(Press the receiver button 4 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 5-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 5 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 10-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 6 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 15-second delay);

(Press the receiver button 7 times: The corresponding relay will close after a 20-second delay).

Step 2

Press the button on the remote control (such as A) once. The indicator light on the receiver will flash and then turn off. The delay mode will be set successfully.

docs.title: (5) Reset function
docs.text: • When the QA-R-011 receiver is reset, all paired transmitters will be unpaired and will no longer be able to control the receiver.

docs.title: How to reset
docs.text: Click the learning button on the receiver 8 times. The indicator light will flash and then will turn off. The reset will be complete.

docs.title: (6) Power-On Auto-Engagement
docs.text: • After this mode is set, the relay will automatically turn on when the receiver is powered back on after a power outage.

docs.title: How to set power-on auto-engagement
docs.text:
Press and hold the receiver's learning button for more than 5 seconds until the indicator light flashes and then goes off. Power-On Auto-Engagement is now set successfully.

(After successful setup, the relay will remain in the energized state when the device is powered back on, regardless of its state before the power outage.)

  • Once the receiving module and transmitter are paired and a working mode is set, the receiving module will keep this mode even after power off and power on again.
  • The following working modes require the use of QIACHIP brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receiving modules/wireless remote control switches). Compatibility with other brands is not guaranteed.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the button on the transmitter once: the output pin of the receiving module will output a high level and maintain this state.
  • Press the same button again: the corresponding output pin of the receiving module will switch to a low level.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
Step 1

After connecting the external learning button, Click the external learning button connected to the receiving module four times. The LED indicator on the receiving module will flash four times, then stay on, and the receiving module will enter the pairing state.

Step 2

Press the button on the transmitter to be paired. The LED indicator on the receiving module will flash and then turn off, indicating that the momentary mode has been set successfully.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the matched button on the transmitter: the output pin of the receiving module outputs high level and remains unchanged.
  • Press the same button again: the corresponding output pin of the receiving module switches to low level.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

After connecting the external learning button, Click the external learning button connected to the receiving module twice. The LED indicator on the receiving module will flash twice, then stay on, and the receiving module will enter the pairing state.

Step 2

Press any button on the transmitter to be paired. The LED indicator on the receiving module will flash continuously and then turn off, indicating that the toggle mode has been set successfully.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • The two buttons on the transmitter control the output pin of the receiving module to output high level and low level respectively.
  • The first configured button can only control the output pin to output high level, and the second configured button can only control the output pin to output low level.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

After connecting the external learning button, Click the external learning button connected to the receiving module three times. The LED indicator on the receiving module will flash three times, then stay on, and the receiving module will enter the pairing state.

Step 2

Press one button on the transmitter to be paired. The LED indicator on the receiving module will flash and then stay on.

Step 3

Press another button on the transmitter to be paired. The LED indicator on the receiving module will flash continuously and then turn off, indicating that the latching mode has been set successfully.

docs.title: (4) Reset function
docs.text: When the RX480E-1A receiver module is reset, all paired transmitters will be unpaired and will no longer be able to control the receiver module.

docs.title: How to reset
docs.text:
Step 1

After connecting the external learning button, click the external learning button connected to the receiving module 8 times. The indicator light will flash 8 times and then turn off. The reset will be complete.

docs.title: (5) Power-On Auto-Engagement
docs.text: After the module is powered off and then powered on again, its output pin directly outputs high level.

docs.title: How to set Power-On Auto-Engagement
docs.text:
Step 1

After connecting an external learning button, press the learning button 6 times. The indicator on the receiving module flashes and then turns off, and the output pin of the receiving module outputs high level.

  • Total straight length of the antenna wire: 170mm

docs.title: Receive mode
docs.text:
When the module is powered on, the red and blue indicators light up for configuration. The indicators turn off when configuration is complete.

  • Two flashes of the red indicator indicate Receive mode.
  • Two flashes of the blue indicator indicate Transmit mode.

(1) Momentary mode; (2) Toggle mode; (3) Latching mode; (4) Reset function.

  • Once the receiving module and transmitter are paired and a working mode is set, the receiving module will keep this mode even after power off and power on again.
  • The following working modes require the use of QIACHIP brand remote controls (transmitters) and controllers (receiving modules/wireless remote control switches). Compatibility with other brands is not guaranteed.

docs.title: (1) Momentary mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press and hold the button on the paired transmitter, and the corresponding output pin on the receiver module will output a high level.
  • Release the button, and the corresponding output pin on the receiver module will switch to a low level.
  • Each time the receiver module receives a signal, the transmitter will receive a feedback signal, and the blue indicator on the transmitter will flash 3 times and then turn off.

docs.title: How to set momentary mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button on the receiver module once. The red indicator on the receiver module will flash once and then stay on, and the receiver module will enter the pairing state

Step 2

Press the button on the transmitter to be paired. If the red indicator on the receiver module flashes 5 times, the pairing is successful.

docs.title: (2) Toggle mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press the button on the paired transmitter once, and the corresponding output pin on the receiver module will output a high level and hold it.
  • Press the same button again, and the corresponding output pin on the receiver module will switch to a low level.
  • Each time the receiver module receives a signal, the transmitter will receive a feedback signal, and the blue indicator on the transmitter will flash 3 times and then turn off.

docs.title: How to set toggle mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button on the receiver module twice. The red indicator on the receiver module will stay on, and the receiver module will enter the pairing state.

Step 2

Press the button on the transmitter module to be paired. If the red indicator on the receiver flashes 5 times, the pairing is successful.

docs.title: (3) Latching mode
docs.text:
In this mode:

  • Press one of the paired transmitter buttons, and the corresponding output pin on the receiver module will output a high level and hold it, while all other output pins will output a low level.
  • If two transmitter buttons are pressed at the same time, all output pins on the receiver module will output a low level and hold it.
  • Each time the receiver module receives a signal, the transmitter will receive a feedback signal, and the blue indicator on the transmitter will flash 3 times and then turn off.

docs.title: How to set latching mode
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button on the receiver module three times. The red indicator on the receiver module will stay on, and the receiver module will enter the pairing state.

Step 2

Press the button on the transmitter to be paired. If the red indicator on the receiver module flashes 5 times, the pairing is successful.

docs.title: (4) Reset function
docs.text: When the RX480E-868 receiver module is reset, all paired transmitters will be unpaired and will no longer be able to control the receiver module.

docs.title: How to reset
docs.text:
Step 1

Click the learning button on the receiver module 8 times. The indicator light will flash 8 times and then turn off. The reset will be complete.

docs.title: Receive to Transmit Function
docs.text: Press the learning button on the receiver module 5 times. The red indicator on the receiver module flashes twice. The module switches from receive mode to transmit mode. This indicates a successful conversion.

docs.title: Transmit to Receive Function
docs.text: Press the learning button on the transmitter module 5 times. The red indicator on the transmitter module flashes 5 times. The module switches from transmit mode to receive mode. After power off and restart, the red indicator on the transmitter module flashes twice. This indicates a successful conversion.

For general applications, the antenna can directly adopt market-available standard specifications. Details are as follows:

  • Antenna core diameter (including outer sheath): 1.0 mm
  • Antenna core diameter (excluding outer sheath): 0.5 mm
  • Wire length at the soldering end: 17.5 mm
  • Wire length at the antenna end: 9.5 mm
  • Antenna winding diameter (including outer sheath): 5 mm
  • Number of winding turns: 15 turns

docs.title: Electrical characteristics
docs.text: Parameter Value Input voltage DC 2.0V-3.6V RF frequency 433.92MHz Power Consumption Current in shutdown mode is less than 0.1μA Maximum Data Rate 20Kbps Maximum Transmit Power 11dBm Working temperature -40~85℃ Size 17.51x13.25x1mm

docs.title: NOTE
docs.text:
1. This product is a CMOS device. Anti-static precautions must be taken during storage, transportation and operation.
  2. Ensure reliable grounding when the device is in use.
